blocks.caption: A sanguinophage in salon form — two hundred years old and still too beautiful

blocks.text: He sits across from you at the bar. Beautiful. Too beautiful — the kind people turn for, who knows they turn, and likes it. He buys you a drink. He smiles. You smile back, because his smile is like a warm bath: pulls you in, relaxes you, switches off the part of your brain that usually says «careful».
He's been dead for two hundred years. But he smells of expensive cologne. And he smiles in a way that makes you forget about it.
Nature

A vampire is undead. A corpse whose soul is dissolved in its blood. Not a virus, not a mutation, not «the next stage of evolution». Sin given flesh. In every vampire there is a struggle — not between man and beast, but between restraint and indulgence. The soul is with him, he tells good from evil. And every day — he chooses. And every day — he loses a little.

Vampires are hedonists. Each has his hang-up: sex, drink, manipulation. The last is the most intellectual and the most dangerous. They love being among people. Hiding among them. They are sociable, charming, sexy — and with age, only more so.
Becoming

She didn't remember when exactly she stopped resisting. The first day — she still held on. Locked herself in the apartment, chewed ice to give her mouth something to do besides scream. Her body burned — not with fever, with desire. She wanted everything: vodka, sex, speed, someone else's pain. Wanted it so badly her nails dug into her palms until they bled.

The second day — she left the apartment. «Just for a walk.» Ended up in a club. Didn't remember how. Three cocktails. A dance with a stranger. His hand on her hip — and something clicked inside her, like a lock opened from outside.

The third day — there wasn't one. Or rather, there was, but she didn't remember it. Came to in someone else's kitchen, in someone else's shirt, with the taste of iron in her mouth. In the sink — pink water.

That night — she died. Quietly, in her sleep, without pain. And an hour later — she opened her eyes. And knew where to go.

**Slow turning:** the vampire bites, injects venom — his essence diluted with the victim's blood. Three days of temptation. Inhibitions drop, passions seethe. Yield — euphoria. Resist — withdrawal. On the third day — death. At night — rising. Blood Bond with the Parent: twenty years of slavery.
**Fast:** the vampire drains dry, injects essence in the last minutes. Rising in ten to fifteen minutes. The struggle is weaker, the bond is weaker — but resisting is also easier.

**If you hold out three days** — a miracle. The Seed of Darkness is not destroyed — it is processed. Full immunity. An ascetic — not from suppression, but because all the temptations have been lived through. All at once. In three days. Such people: three in a hundred.
Blood as Currency
The soul is dissolved in the blood. You can kill a vampire by draining all his blood and letting it dry. Don't let it — and he'll absorb it back.
**Reserve:** 5 litres in the body + up to 5 in the stomach. A hard ceiling. The ancients keep extra outside — a «blood bank», a bubble orbiting the body.

**Expenditure:** ~100 ml per day just to «live». Imitating breathing: another 100. Food: 100 per meal (it isn't digested — you have to consciously switch the intestines on). Sex: 100 per night. Combat mode: 100 per MINUTE. Body temperature (from 30°C to normal): 50/day.
**Thirst** — after losing more than a litre and a half. At four — torpor: hibernation, expenditure drops to a drop a day.
Combat Mode

Speed and strength × 10. An energy field along the epidermis — thin, tough as ferroconcrete. Pierce it and the bullet behaves as in a normal body. With eyes closed, he sees with the astral body. The ancients: ranged blood-sucking (10–15 m, through pores), stopping bleeding, blood as nanomachines, change of appearance (a month — face, three — body).
Weaknesses

**Fire** — burns like a log, blood like kerosene. **Decapitation** — instant death (one surviving capillary is enough — if the blood is connected to the head, he's alive). **Sunlight** — from discomfort to spontaneous combustion, depending on the fall from grace. **Symbols of faith** — likewise. **Garlic** — heightened sense of smell, unbearable stench. **Aspen** — bleeding (a young one it'll kill, an old one will tear it out and shove it back in). **Scattered grain** — works: perfectionism. **Enchanted cold steel** — ignores the field, works as on an ordinary body. Hunters use sabres and katanas (swords are too slow for a vampire in combat mode). **Stylism** — ruin his clothes and he'll snap.
Society

Families. The Lesser Scarlet Council (city), the General (country), the Great (world). One vampire per hundred thousand people. «The free» — outside the Councils. Vendetta — total liquidation of the family. Hypnosis — not telepathy, but blood dispersed by airborne droplets.

Immortal integrators: millennia-old, having found balance. They work as confectioners and doctors. They have families. They even go out in the sun. If they fully overcome sin — they burn. What waits in the afterlife — no one knows. Vampire souls are not summoned. Nobody wants to check.
Upir (Thrall)

An upir is a person who, of their own free will, asked to be enslaved. At first it seems a good deal: the vampire feeds the thrall micro-doses of his own corruption — not enough to kill or turn, just enough to make the body start changing. Ageing slows. Stamina, healing, strength — all creep upward. Ten years of such service and a forty-year-old upir looks and works like a twenty-five-year-old athlete.

The price hides not in the body but in the personality — and is paid so slowly the upir doesn't notice the deductions. The corruption year by year files down what made him him: tastes, stubbornness, attachments, conscience. Devotion, which began as a conscious choice, becomes chemical dependence — the upir serves now not because he decided to, but because without his master, withdrawal begins. He is a slave who still remembers he was once free, but has forgotten what it tasted like. And — worst of all — almost always perfectly content.
Vurdalak

blocks.caption: A vurdalak — a body drained dry and discarded, raised by dark emanations

blocks.text: A vurdalak begins with carelessness. The vampire drank a person dry, threw the body away without bothering to dispose of it — and into the emptied flesh, like a draught through an open window, dark emanations of the Twilight flow in and lift it to its feet. This isn't resurrection. It's an imprint: the dead brain holds memory of roughly the last year of life, and the vurdalak wears it for a while like someone else's coat — a pale, inexact copy of who the deceased used to be, and at the same time hellishly, insatiably hungry.

The vurdalak's hunger is of a special kind. It feeds not on blood — on life itself. Every hundred millilitres sucked ages the victim by five years: a teenager after one meeting with a vurdalak can become an old man, and then crumble to dust. Sunlight the vurdalak doesn't fear at all — it is immune. The longer it exists, the less of the human donor remains in it and the more distinctly emerges the personality of the Guest — that very emanation-spirit that raised it.

For a vampire, the appearance of a vurdalak is both shame and evidence. It means someone in the Family ate sloppily and left filth behind. For such carelessness the Scarlet Council can execute: the Families do not tolerate the leaving of walking proof behind them.
Savan and Buka

blocks.caption: A Savan — an emanation-spirit without flesh; pulls warmth and will like a draught through the soul

blocks.text: Kill an old vurdalak — and you have not finished the matter, only turned the page. From the disintegrating body rises that same Guest, the emanation-spirit, now without flesh. Its first form is the **Savan**: a translucent floating veil, a scrap of cold air in the shape of a man. Physical damage the Savan inflicts not at all — there is nothing for the hands to catch. But it pulls the warmth, will, and joy out of the victim; standing near it is like standing in a draught that blows directly into the soul. A Dementor on the lowest setting. Savans are cowards and panic at sacred symbols — a cross, sincere prayer, blessed silver drive them off.

But a Savan that was allowed to feed unhindered, and that drained a victim entirely, grows into the next form — the **Buka**. The Buka chooses children. It comes in the hour between sleep and waking, settles on the chest with sleep paralysis, and feeds on terror — grows the nightmares and drinks them, night after night, until less and less of the child remains in the bed. There is a weapon against the Buka, and it is simple: the «Our Father» read with genuine, undoubted faith strikes this creature like an ion cannon. The trouble is only that genuine faith is rarer than the Bukas themselves.

blocks.caption: Beside a night highway — the hitchhiker you shouldn't have picked up

blocks.text: She stands by the side of a night highway. Or at a club bar where the bass beats your ribs. Or knee-deep in a forest lake, head thrown back to the moon, water sliding down her collarbones — to where you're already looking.
You're already looking.

A mavka isn't a monster. A mavka is a sentence. Not passed on you. Passed on her — long ago, in another life, in another body, when she was an ordinary girl, and the ordinary girl believed an ordinary scumbag, and the ordinary scumbag betrayed her.
How they are born

Always the same way. Always — a girl for whom THIS love was the last. Ugly, or lonely, or simply the one the world had ignored too long. The one who put everything into the relationship — no reserve, no insurance, no Plan B. And got back emptiness. Public humiliation. Photos in the group chat. Laughter. «It was a prank, you fool.»
She goes home. Locks the door. Takes out a rope. Or opens a window. Or fills the bathtub.
And the moment her body begins to die — the world freezes. Literally: the second hand stops, dust hangs in the air, and in the absolute silence she hears a voice.
Not devil's, not thunderous. Quiet. Tired. Familiar — as if it were the voice of a girlfriend she never had.
The voice promises no heaven. Promises no happiness. Promises one thing: that the one who did this will pay. And that she will be beautiful. For the first time in her life — beautiful.
If she says «yes» — the old body falls. Dead. Empty. And she — is no longer here. She is in a new body. Perfect, impossible, made of darkness and grief and tears that will never be shed again.
Because mavkas don't cry.
What they look like

An embodiment. Not of beauty — of desire. A mavka looks exactly the way every man within a hundred and fifty kilometres WANTS to see her. Not concretely — averaged out, from the sum of fantasies, magazine covers, screens. The beauty standard incarnated in living flesh, smoothly drifting with time: in the early last century she was slight, with boyish hips and a short cut. Today — long legs, heavy breasts, wasp waist, magazine-cover face, lips that dry your mouth out. In twenty years the standard will shift — and she will shift with it, imperceptibly, like a clock hand.
The hair — only two colours. Black, like the bottom of the well she once looked into. Or gold, like the field she walked home through — that last time. There is no third option.

And she is invulnerable. Not like a vampire, who can be pierced if you hit hard enough. A mavka is a wall. A bullet enters her and… nothing. No wound. No blood. No impact. A grenade throws her — she gets up. Smiling. The same smile that dried your mouth out five minutes ago.
How they hunt

blocks.caption: The alley behind the club, the dead street lamp — and a hand at the throat

blocks.text: She approaches. Smiles. Says something — it doesn't matter what, you won't remember the words anyway, only the voice, only the scent, only the feeling that the air around her is warmer, thicker, sweeter. You follow her, because not following is impossible. Not physically — every cell of your body simply knows that THERE will be better than HERE.

The mavka intuitively picks the worst. The dirtier the man's soul — the brighter he glows in her perception. The rapist. The liar. The one who broke hearts like matchsticks — incidentally, without even noticing.
But a regular one can stumble in too. The mavka doesn't check passports.

And afterwards — she kills. Not quickly. Not mercifully. With the cruelty that has been gathering since the moment the photos appeared on other people's phones, and a body in a noose swayed in an empty apartment.

Club «Zero», Saturday, two in the morning. Dima — twenty-six, gym body, barbershop, BMW in the parking lot — spotted her immediately. They don't exist. They just don't: legs, breasts, face, a dress with less fabric than his handkerchief. She looked at him — and Dima went. No questions. No plan. His legs carried him.

Behind the club — an alley. The street lamp was dead. She leaned against the wall, pulled him by the belt. Dima pressed into her, hands everywhere: ass, breasts, hips. She moaned — quiet, hot, straight into his ear. He pulled up her hem.

And suddenly — her hand. On his throat. Not embracing — squeezing. Fingers that a second ago had stroked his nape — became steel. Dima choked, tried to pry her hand off. Couldn't. With both hands. Couldn't.

She lifted him by the throat with one hand. Like a kitten. Pressed him to the wall — the same one she had leaned against. The same motion. Almost tender.

«Run,» she said. And let go.

Dima ran. Through the courtyard, over the fence, through the construction site. His heart hammered so hard he heard it in his ears. He ran for three minutes — and began to believe he'd escaped.

She was standing at the end of the alley he ran into. Hands on hips. Smiling. The same smile that dried his mouth out ten minutes ago.

«More?» she asked.

Dima was identified by DNA and his dental chart. The face didn't survive.
Four nights

And still — not everything is lost for a mavka. Because the girl's soul — the real one, who believed and loved and broke — is not dead. She sleeps. Deep inside the machine built of grievance. Like a passenger in a car driven by rage.
The soul can be woken.
**Night one: silence.**

You didn't reach for her. Didn't put a hand on her thigh. Didn't try to lead her to a room. You — talked. Asked. Listened. And somewhere near the end of the second hour — something flickered in her eyes that shouldn't have been there. Confusion. For a second — through the perfect mask — peeked out the girl who used to paint board-game miniatures and feed the stray cat behind the dorms.
She'll tell you things she doesn't know herself. Fragments of the past slipping out without her will — like words of a foreign language that you understand but can't remember where you learned.
Before dawn she'll tell you the truth. Who she is. What she does to men. And she'll ask you not to come back.
**Night two: the gift.**
You came. With a paint set — because she let it slip. Or with a book. Or with a photograph of that very cat. With something that belongs not to the mavka — but to the girl inside.

She'll look at the gift. Then at you. And she will cry. Mavkas don't cry — but this one will, because for a second she'll be not a mavka but Lena, or Masha, or Aigul, or whoever she was before the noose.
She'll embrace you. You can embrace back. To kiss — no. «You haven't earned it yet. I want your kiss to be real.»

And all night you will talk. She'll recall how she became a mavka. She'll tell you — stumbling, getting lost, swallowing her words. You only need to listen. And stroke her head — carefully, the way you stroke a child who had a bad dream.
**Night three: fire.**
She'll stop crying. She'll stop talking. She'll look at you — and there will be no girl in that look. Only the mavka. Only a body made of desire, every movement saying: take me.
She will do everything. Everything you want, and much of what you didn't know you wanted. Not forced — willingly, tenderly, desperately. As if her life depended on it.
Not yielding is possible. Physically. But every second will cost as much will as an ordinary man spends in a year. Because in front of you stands — literally — the ideal. Warm, alive, asking.
You held out — hysterics. Tears. Screaming. Little fists on your chest. «I disgust you! Leave!»
Leave. She'll stand in the doorway watching you go. And if you look back — you'll see not a mavka. A girl. Frightened, in tears, not understanding what just happened.
**Night four: silence again.**
She comes by herself. After midnight. Quietly, like snow. You won't hear her — you'll feel her. Lips on your forehead. Trembling fingers on your cheek.
Then — confession.

If she's twenty years old — that's ten names. If she's a thousand — she won't finish before dawn. Each name — a face, a voice, the last sound. She remembers them all. And she tells you, choking, because if she doesn't tell it now — she never will.
Listen. Wrap her in a blanket. Pour her tea — ordinary, with sugar. Hold her close. Don't let go.
When she falls silent — she'll look you in the eye. And you will see both: the mavka and the girl, the predator and the prey, a thousand years of solitude and one minute of hope.
«I love you.»
Three words. And behind them — an abyss. One direction — life. The other — death. For both of you.
What comes after

blocks.caption: The fourth night passed, the first real kiss — a mavka became human

blocks.text: If you answer — the world will change. Quietly, without flashes, without thunder. In that moment you will simply know: this is true. This was always true. You love her — as you have loved no one. And you will love — always.

The first real kiss — and a mavka becomes human. Not at once: slowly, like a sunrise. Her skin will warm. Her breath will quicken. Her heart — already beating — will beat differently. As if for the first time.

Anton was a programmer. Thirty-two, a small beard, glasses, a T-shirt with the Linux penguin. Not ugly — just nobody. The kind of man women don't notice, the way they don't notice the furniture.

Two in the morning, the Leningrad Highway past Zelenograd. She stood at the kerb — alone, in a dress that didn't conceal so much as point the way, in shoes you can't walk in. Anton stopped because it was two in the morning, a woman alone, who knew what could happen.

She got in. A scent — warm, sweet, the kind that catches the breath. Endless legs in stockings that didn't even pretend to hide anything.

«Lonely, handsome?»

«No, I… do you need help? It's late, cold.»

She leaned toward him — and the strap slid off her shoulder. By accident. Or not. Her breast — perfect, heavy — was suddenly twenty centimetres from his face. Anton stared at the windshield.

«Take me to Rechnoy. Since that's not why you stopped.»

They drove. She leaned back in the seat, crossed one leg over the other — the skirt rode up so far that Anton mechanically noted: no underwear, and what was there was perfection itself. He shifted gear and kept looking at the road.

After ten minutes of silence she stopped fidgeting. Looked at him — no longer with a huntress's eyes, but with something like surprise.

«You really don't want to?»

«That's not it. Listen, you're a beautiful woman. How does someone like you end up on this road? You shouldn't be standing by the highway — you should be in films.»

A pause. A long one.

«What do you care?» Her voice — sharp, prickly.

«I do.»

Another pause. Shorter.

«You're strange.»

«People say that a lot.»

She laughed. Not the way before — not with the seductive smile from her arsenal. For real. A short crooked laugh that didn't suit that perfect face — and was therefore the first genuine emotion of the evening.

«As a kid I built ship models. Sailing ships. You know — the Black Pearl, the Flying Dutchman… I had twelve of them. On the shelf. In a row.»

She didn't understand why she'd said it. The words came on their own — from deep down, from a place the mavka didn't know.

«Really? I built them too. The Cutty Sark. Started three times, never finished — not enough patience.»

She turned to him. Stopped fidgeting, stopped showing off. Sat with her knees drawn up, hugging them — and in that pose, in that gesture, something came through that shouldn't exist in a mavka. Something childlike. Something real.

«And I loved Star Trek. Every season. Original, Next Generation, even Voyager…»

Anton nearly lost the wheel. «Voyager? Really? Even season four?»

«Especially season four.»

They reached Rechnoy. She didn't get out. He didn't ask. He turned around and drove back — slowly, along the empty night highway. They talked — about Picard and Sisko, about why Discovery is a betrayal of the idea, about which episode is best (hers — «The Inner Light», his — «The Measure of a Man»). They passed Zelenograd. Turned around again. There was a cat waiting at home, but the cat had an auto-feeder.

Around four in the morning she went quiet. Mid-sentence — just went quiet. Looked straight ahead. Then turned to him.

«Do you know today is your second birthday?»

Anton didn't understand.

«I'll tell you a story. About a girl.»

Her voice changed. Got quieter. Hollow. As if the words came from a place that had long been boarded up.

«There lived a girl. Tall, broad-shouldered — heavy bones, slow metabolism, her mother was the same. At university they called her Mrs Doubtfire. She pretended it was funny. She drew comics in her notebook — Kirk, Spock, the Enterprise. Built twelve sailing-ship models on the shelf. Rewatched Star Trek in a loop. No friends. Boys — even less. Who would want her, really?»

Silence in the car. Just the engine.

«Then a boy showed up. Handsome. Sat next to her in the library, looked into her notebook. ‘Oh! Kirk! I prefer Picard, actually.' The girl nearly died. A guy who looks like a model — and knows the difference?»

«Three weeks. Dates, walks, coffee. The girl was happy. For the first time. Came to lectures on his arm, and everyone — EVERYONE — stared. At her. At Mrs Doubtfire. And she glowed.»

She went quiet. Anton waited.

«Then — the night. The girl's first night ever. In the morning — photos in the year-group chat. Videos. His message: ‘Bet for a grand. The guys didn't believe me. Nothing personal.' He'd learned the terminology on purpose. Kirk. Picard. Prepared.»

Anton's knuckles went white on the wheel.

«The girl came home. Took out a rope…»

A pause. A long one.

«And at the moment her feet left the stool — time stopped. And someone offered her another life.»

She turned to him. Lifted her right hand — slowly, the way you show a trick to a child. Before his eyes the nails grew to five centimetres — black, smooth, sharp as scalpels. For a second he stared at them. For a second — they slid back. Her hand was perfect again, manicured.

«That girl was me,» she said quietly. «Before I died.»

Silence.

«You were supposed to be dinner, Anton. I came out to the road to hunt. You stopped — and didn't grab. You asked how I'd ended up like this. In… I don't even know how many years — you're the first who asked.»

Outside, dawn.

«You're not like them. You've twisted my whole head. So — live. I want you to be happy. And don't ever come back to where you picked me up. Never. I mean it.»

She got out at Rechnoy — barefoot, shoes in her hand. Walked away without looking back.

Anton came back the next night. With a model-build kit for the Black Pearl. She was standing in the same spot. As if she'd been waiting. She saw the box — and her face… Mavkas don't cry. This one cried. Held the box to her chest like a child. Then buried her face in his chest — and cried the way those who have forgotten how cry.

He embraced her. She lifted her face for a kiss — wet, mascara smudged, beautiful.

«No,» Anton said. «Not yet. I want you to kiss me when you're absolutely sure you want to. Not from gratitude. From love.»

She opened her mouth. Closed it. Opened it again. Couldn't find the words. For the first time in a very, very long while — couldn't find the words.

On the third night he came again. She was waiting — and this time she had nothing on but his jacket, which he'd left the time before. Four hours in the car. The windows fogged. She did everything — everything a perfect body made of desire can do. Anton stared at the windshield, gripping the wheel, and thought about sailing ships. About the Cutty Sark he never finished. About each piece of rigging that has to be tied separately.

She left in tears. He drove home and threw up in the sink from the strain.

On the fourth night — she was in his apartment. He didn't know how she got in. He woke from a touch — lips on his forehead, light as a butterfly. She sat on the edge of the bed. In his sweater with the Linux penguin. The sweater reached her knees.

«There were a hundred and forty-three,» she said quietly. «I remember every one.»

Anton sat up. Turned on the kettle. She talked — long, hoarse, stumbling. He listened. Made tea — three times, because she forgot to drink, and he forgot he'd already poured.

Before morning she fell silent. Looked at him — the look you can't hide from, because it goes not into the eyes but into the soul.

«I love you. I know I have no right. But I love you.»

Anton — thirty-two, small beard, glasses, sweater with the Linux penguin — looked at the most beautiful woman in the world. At a woman who had killed a hundred and forty-three people. At the girl who used to draw Kirk in a notebook and cried on his chest.

«I love you too,» he said.

He wasn't sure. Not at all. But when the words came out — he knew it was true. That it had always been true. He just hadn't known the words.

She kissed him. The first real kiss — and the world became different.
She will stay beautiful. Forever. But the beauty will stop being a weapon — it will simply be her. Combat ability remains — briefly, for defence. Invulnerability — gone.

She will love you the way only those who know what it's like to be unloved can love. Absolutely. Unconditionally. Until the last breath, which will coincide with yours — because your souls are bound now, and one cannot exist without the other.

She will be jealous — furiously, hopelessly, to tears. Of every woman, every glance. Not from anger — from fear. Because she lost everything once before, and that fear isn't going anywhere, even through a thousand years of happiness.

She adapts instantly. Becomes whoever you need. Not from submission — from desire. Your world is her world. Your interests are hers. If you're a programmer — within a year she'll be writing code. If a hunter — stripping a rifle. If just a quiet man who likes tea in the evenings — she'll sit beside you, knees drawn up, in silence. Because sometimes silence shared is everything you need.

And if anyone tries to hurt her — they will very quickly discover that an ex-mavka is still a mavka. Just with the hunting mode switched off. Switching it back on takes a second. And God help whoever pressed that button.

blocks.caption: Noon, shadows are shortest — and there is nowhere to run

blocks.text: If the fourth night ends in refusal — the girl's soul dies. Finally. The body of the mavka remains — empty, like a house with knocked-out windows. And into that emptiness a demon enters.

What results is not a mavka. Not a woman. Not a human. A terrible, broken creature with arms down to its knees and claws that leave furrows in stone. The face — a caricature of female beauty: sagging skin, empty eyes, a mouth that opens wider than is possible. It attacks at noon — when shadows are shortest and there is nowhere to run.

All the combat qualities of a mavka — the speed of a cheetah, ten-metre leaps, fifty-metre running jumps — plus demonic rage and teleportation. A mavka under threat will more likely flee. A Poludenitsa will charge straight at you. To tear. To break. To kill.
They are rare, and thank God for it. Vulnerable to symbols of faith: a demon is a demon, no matter how deep it has buried itself in someone else's body.

blocks.caption: Not a rope, but water: a drowned girl in service to the Vodyanoy

blocks.text: Another noose. Not a rope — water. Girls who drowned themselves over unhappy love. Their souls are taken by the Vodyanoy — master of the body of water, an ancient being who needs servants.

A rusalka is undead — but breathes. Warm. Beautiful — with that cold riverine beauty that gives goosebumps not from arousal but from foreboding. Can conceive a child — but a marriage to a rusalka is doomed: she will always be drawn back to the depths. Literally. She knows it herself. She suffers from it herself. And can do nothing — the Vodyanoy pulls.

When the master is awake — the rusalki work: lure travellers, drown them. Looseness, nudity, calling voice — none of it is character, all of it is tool. When the Vodyanoy sleeps — they get autonomy. And then you see who they really are: some go wild, using the freedom; some sit quietly on the bank, knees drawn up, looking at the water that took everything.
A rusalka can be killed. She will be reborn in the nearest body of water. The only way to free them is to kill the Vodyanoy. Then all his rusalki are free. And dead. For good.
Freedom and death — one word. For a rusalka, the same thing.

blocks.caption: When the master sleeps, the rusalka gets autonomy — and you see who she really is
A soul locked into a desiccated body by necro-symbiont bandages

blocks.text:
He lay on the stone table and stared at the ceiling. Torches flickered. The priests sang — a long, monotonous, endless song that made the bones of his skull vibrate. He felt every touch of the knife. Every cut. Every organ leaving the body — warm, wet, unexpectedly heavy, as if it clung to its master and didn't want to go.

The pain was beyond words. Beyond screaming — he screamed for the first twenty minutes, then his voice gave out. Beyond unconsciousness — something in the song wouldn't let him lose it. He had to live through this with open eyes.

When they began winding on the bandages — the pain did not go. It changed. Became… his own. The bandages were alive: warm, damp, they sank into the parched flesh and became part of the body, like skin atop skin. And with every wrap — the world changed. Grew clearer. Sharper. Colder.

The last thing he felt as a man — a tear. It rolled down his cheek and was absorbed into a bandage. The bandage pulsed in answer. Warmly. Like a mother's hand.

Then feeling ended. Only understanding remained.

A mummy is a human soul locked inside a desiccated body by bandages — necro-symbionts. A variety of artificial lich. The bandages are not cloth. They are a necro-biological symbiont, made by ancient sorcerers: a living being that fuses with the body, locks the soul inside it, and many times amplifies the carrier's magical abilities.
You cannot turn just anyone. From a weak soul comes a servant: a maddened-from-pain corpse barely remembering its own name. From a strong soul comes a mighty lich — surpassing the ordinary ones.

**Vulnerability:** destruction of 51% of the bandages = the soul tears free → instant afterlife. The bandages regenerate (grow back like flesh, ~10 years for a full new set). The body itself does not.

**Temperament:** not aggressive. Can be friendly. Pure ratio rules them. The young — almost human (emotions muted). The ancient — robots. As an ally — reliable: bargain struck, won't betray (if there is no rational sense in betraying). They do not band into groups. Each one — individual.
Egyptian mummies — some are alive. From those very centuries. Silent, patient, infinitely rational beings who have outlived all empires and will outlive all the rest.

blocks.caption: A Lich — a mage who walked the road of necromancy to its end; soul in a phylactery

blocks.text: A Lich is a mage who walked the road of necromancy so long and so far that the road one day ended. There was nowhere further to go — and then the ritual began.

It takes years of preparation. First the mage creates a phylactery — a vessel into which the soul will be transferred. Then he weaves a complex web of binding spells, an error in any of which means not death but something worse. And at the very end he takes a special poison. The body dies — truly, finally — and the soul, at the moment of death, flows into the phylactery. Resembling sokushinbutsu, the Buddhist practice of lifetime self-mummification: the same cold, voluntary, calculated dying for the sake of what comes after.
The phylactery

The Lich keeps the phylactery separate from the body — but not too far: the connection between soul and vessel has a finite range. This is his one true vulnerability — and at the same time his guarantee. Destroy the phylactery, and the Lich dies instantly and finally. But this is not a Horcrux from fairy tales: the soul cannot be broken into pieces and scattered across the world, the phylactery is always one. Finding it is the whole task. Solving the task is almost impossible: the Lich has hidden his heart for centuries and thought about that hiding-place better than you can.
Body — a glove

His own dead body is, for the Lich, only the first of the shells. He moves it by the telekinesis of his soul, like a puppet on invisible strings; this body feels no pain, knows no fear. And when the shell wears out or burns in combat, the Lich, within his range, seizes another dead body — strictly dead, the living he cannot occupy — and puts it on like a new glove.

A Lich has no emotions, and not because the ritual burned them out. They weren't there even before the ritual: only an ancient, mighty mage, long gone cold, can walk this path to its end. A warm man turns aside earlier.

**Difference from a mummy.** A mummy — a soul locked by bandages in one body, amplified by a symbiont: she is stronger magically, but chained to her flesh. A Lich — a soul in a phylactery, free, changing bodies like clothes: he is weaker in pure magic than a mummy, but incomparably more mobile. A mummy must be overcome. From a Lich, you have to manage not to fall behind.
Not every dead man who doesn't rest quietly is a monster. Sometimes death leaves behind not a creature but an imprint. Incorporeal forms are what a person becomes when he doesn't fully leave.
Remnant

A Remnant is a living photograph of someone deceased. A psychoactive cast, an imprint that a place once took from a person and still holds. The Remnant walks the same corridor, sits in the same chair, repeats the same gesture — for years, for centuries, unchanging, not noticing you. It is not sentient. It is not dangerous. Strictly speaking, it isn't even undead — the undead at least wants something. The Remnant wants nothing. It is just an echo of footsteps in an empty corridor that still won't fade.
Revenant

A Revenant has truly and fully come back. He breathes, walks, eats, feels, remembers himself. From the side — a living person, and he almost believes it himself. He was raised by the powers of Heaven or Hell to finish the unfinished: avenge, save, defend, say. Revenants are raised only for achievable tasks — the higher powers do not waste force on the wind. When the task is done, the Revenant dies again, quietly and without pain: his term was bound to the deed, and the deed is closed. You can kill a Revenant before his time — he is mortal; whether he comes back depends on those who raised him and on how badly they still need his work.
Einherjar

An Einherjar is a Revenant taken to the limit. The same risen man with the same unfinished mission, but endowed with a power that chills the blood. Colossal regeneration — wounds close before your eyes. Monstrous endurance — he absorbs damage that would drop a platoon, and keeps walking. The closest familiar image: the Crow from the old film — a man who has come back to do one single thing and cannot be stopped until it is done. Killing an Einherjar is a task for an entire well-coordinated, well-armed group. If he is killable at all — which, as a rule, you only find out as you go.

blocks.caption: An Alva of the High Court — a solar knight of the Tuatha Dé Danann

blocks.text: She is beautiful in a way the living are not. Not «a beautiful woman» — beautiful as a lightning strike is beautiful, or the last light over the sea: from such beauty one wants not to possess but to drop to one's knees. You have almost dropped already. That is her first joke. You haven't realised it's a joke. She is already laughing.

An Alva looks down on you — but not with contempt. Contempt has to be earned: to despise someone, one must first acknowledge they exist. She looks at you the way you look at an ant across a path: with cool curiosity — will it turn aside if you lay a twig in its way?
Who they are

Tuatha Dé Danann — the People of the goddess Danu, the high fae, lords of Arcadia-the-Beyond. Singly, each of them is an Alva. They call us Fomorians, by the name of an ancient defeated foe, the way we throw the word «vandal». An Alva is older than a forest. Not figuratively: he remembers this tree as a seed, remembers the hill before the tree. A century to him is an evening. An oath given a thousand years ago binds him as tightly as one given this morning.
Non-human mind

Alvas are neither mad nor stupid — they are simply built on other axes. The human mind rests on cause, guilt, mercy. The Alva mind rests on pattern, symmetry, debt, name, oath, beauty. A broken bargain is, to him, a real wound. A dead child is an abstraction. An Alva does not know how to lie: a lie is a crack in the pattern, and the pattern is what he is. But truth on the lips of a fae is a blade. He will tell you only true things — and arrange those truths so that you destroy yourself, and he will admire your work, because you did it, and he only… spoke.
The Seelie Court — not the same as good

The Tuatha Dé Danann are the summer, Seelie court. You hear «Seelie» and exhale. Don't. Seelie does not mean good. Seelie means they keep form. They will not lie to you. They will perform the bargain to its last letter. Under their roof they will not harm a guest. They are flawlessly, exquisitely courteous — and courtesy is not kindness; courtesy is the rules of a game, and you are not a player in it, you are the board. The Seelie court will do the cruel thing to you — and will be polite while doing it, and will arrange it so that by the letter of a law you never read, the fault is yours. The Unseelie court simply doesn't waste effort on courtesy. That is the only difference. Those who escaped the Seelie envy no one — but they pity the ones who met the Unseelie.
The cruel joke

Fae are not villains: villainy needs hatred, and hatred needs you to matter. You do not matter to them. They amuse themselves. And what amuses a fae is a cruel joke — an elegant one, well-timed, where a Fomorian suffers and the suffering has shape. Your ruin is the punchline, and a good punchline an Alva genuinely values. This is why help from a fae is more dangerous than harm: if a fae has granted your wish, somewhere the trap is being assembled, and the help was only the first half of the joke.
Glamour

Glamour is an illusion woven so finely that it deceives not only the eye but instruments. It falls from one thing only — the touch of cold iron. Under glamour lies the true shape, and it almost never looks like the mask.
Names, oaths, debts

A true name gives power over a fae — which is why they never give theirs. An oath spoken, an Alva will not break: not «won't», cannot. Debts they reckon savagely and remember forever. Do not thank a fae: «thank you» is an admission of debt, and a debt to a fae is a leash. Do not take their food. Do not give your name. And never, under any circumstances, strike a bargain with a fae — even if it seems you're winning. Especially if it seems so.
Cold iron

*Iarann fuar* — cold iron, metal that fire has not touched: it is obtained by repeated cold forging. To the unholy it is poison; to an Alva of the High Court it is an ontological poison — not a wound, an unmaking. Their armour is forged to turn cold iron aside. But armour has gaps — bare skin on arms, belly, thighs. Those are the targets. The Alva leaves them bare on purpose: so that you see — he does not believe you can hit them. To drive cold iron into a hand's-width of bare skin on a creature that moves faster than your eye — the Hunters spend years learning this, and most often die without finishing the lesson.
Combat

Solar blades — blades of condensed light. The Tuatha Dé Danann are called solar knights. They fight in forms from a heroic age that ended before Rome — and the forms still win, because the swordsman is older, faster, and does not fear death as you fear it. Ranged weapons are almost useless: enchantments turn bullets aside in flight. The answer is cold-iron ammunition. After that, see above: hit.
Leprechauns

Not all fae are solar knights. The leprechaun is one of the lesser folk of the Beyond: in honest combat no stronger than a five-year-old, and won't tangle with Fomorians in the open. His weapon is the bargain. Strike any agreement with a leprechaun and, by the twisted laws of the fae, he gains complete power over you, and within that bargain his magic is greater than any Alva's. Leprechauns are the finest hagglers the world has known. The flair and business luck of certain wealthy men comes from that kind of deal. And the joke is still running.
Changelings

blocks.caption: A changeling: the fae is old, and his age betrays him

blocks.text: A changeling is a fae who has taken someone else's place. A magical construct or a minor fae under glamour, it replaces a human — most often an infant — with perfect mimicry: neither mother nor doctor will tell the difference. To identify a changeling without a clairvoyant is nearly impossible — but fae are old, and their age betrays them. A changeling cannot abide a wrongly-shaped thing and is proud of his age: stage something sufficiently absurd in his presence — boil water in an eggshell, fish in an aquarium — and the ancient thing cannot keep silent, will speak up, will give itself away.

An ORPP negotiator placed an eggshell on the stove, poured water into it, and waited. The infant in the cradle followed him with a non-childlike gaze. On the fifth shell the infant couldn't bear it and said in a bass: «I am older than the trees in the yard of this house — and for the first time I see water being boiled in a shell.» «Got you,» said the negotiator. The changeling realised it had betrayed itself — and shrieked, no longer trying to make the sound childlike.
Sometimes a changeling carries inside itself a «seed of the Gate» — a bomb on a timer. They replace people a day or two before activation. If you didn't catch it in time — too late.
Arcadia

Their realm — the Beyond, the Underside of the world. A beautiful land: knights, wonders, an eternal soft light. And behind the beauty — a knife, the way a knife hides behind a bouquet. They take Fomorians there as blood-slaves; time flows wrongly there — you return after what seems a week and don't find alive any of the people you knew. At Beltane the border between worlds thins, and the fae go a little mad: that is when they plant changelings, when people walk into a ring of stones — and don't come out.

blocks.caption: A Child of Noon — complete transformation: a mouth to the ears, an alien mind

blocks.text:
The village was quiet. Too quiet for midday in the desert — no barking dogs, no children's shrieks, no creak of the well's swing-beam. The archaeologist Conrad wrote it down to the heat and walked to the central well. The stones around the well were scratched — long, parallel grooves, as if someone had scraped them with claws. Conrad crouched, ran a finger along a groove. Fresh.

He turned around when the sun was at zenith. In the doorway of the nearest house stood a man. Or what had once been a man: earthen-green skin covered in scales, a mouth stretched from ear to ear in a «smile» that bared two rows of teeth. The eyes — yellow, with vertical pupils — looked at Conrad with an expression he would have identified as curiosity, had he not been busy trying not to die.

Noon. Apogee. Conrad was lucky: he ran faster than the sebekit expected. But the village Conrad never found again. Not on the map, not in the terrain. As if it had never been.

Sebekites are reasoning (with caveats) reptiloids of Mesopotamia and North Africa. Bearers of a curse known as «Solar Eclipse», a curse with no cure and no fooling around. They are at once victims and carriers of their own disease — and that makes them especially dangerous.
Three stages of transformation

blocks.caption: A Child of Dawn — scales and a vertical pupil, the human still showing through

blocks.text: The curse develops slowly, over years, in three stages. **Child of Sunset** — almost human: claws, faintly yellowed eyes with a barely visible vertical pupil, dry skin with subcutaneous growths. You will pass one on the street and not turn. **Child of Dawn** — scales, an obvious vertical pupil, a transparent third eyelid; emotions are hypertrophied and obey no human logic: laughter where weeping should be, rage where there should be relief. **Child of Noon** — complete transformation: an earthen-green scaly hide, a mouth stretched from ear to ear in two rows of teeth, and a mind that is no longer human in any part. Such a one can kill without emotion — and then laugh, peal upon peal, over the corpse.
Rhythm of the day

The sebekit is subject to the sun. In the evening he is calm, at night he falls into stasis — almost safe, some have spent the night in the same room and walked away alive. In the morning he begins to grow savage. By noon he reaches apogee: the higher and hotter the sun, the more savage and faster he is. In the desert at high summer the sebekit is many times more dangerous than at night.
The Curse of Eclipse

The most terrible thing about them is not their teeth. Before death a sebekit curses the witness. All who killed him and all who stood by are infected: the curse slowly, over years, begins turning them into the same. First — an unaccountable pull toward the sun. Then — the desire to scratch the skin until it bleeds, because something is growing under it. Then — the Pilgrimage: a man still considering himself human walks on foot, wearing his feet bloody, back to the place of infection, and nothing can stop him. Kill the migrant on the road, and the curse simply hops to the one who killed him. A closed loop. An unsolvable problem — which is why ORPP is instructed: sebekites are not to be destroyed but isolated.
Eclipse

Once every twelve years, on the day of a great solar eclipse, the sebekites celebrate a holiday humanity is not invited to. They mask as people — at the Sunset stage this comes easily — and infiltrate cities. On the day of the Eclipse a slaughter takes place. This is their way of breeding: every witness to an attacker's death is infected with the curse. A local crocodile apocalypse, after which children with a slight yellowness in their eyes are born in the city for a long time. ORPP prepares for the Eclipse in advance — and every time fails to handle it completely.
Weaknesses

Two of them, and both amusing for a reptile. **Night** — the sebekit falls into torpor, any blow lands. **Water** — the sebekit cannot swim; a deep pond is a wall to him. Otherwise — long-lived: 200–600 years, many older than most of the states they live in.

blocks.caption: A colony of single-celled creatures with one mind — has learned to pretend it's furniture

blocks.text: Take a room in a cheap hotel, throw your bag on a chair — and perhaps in the morning you'll find neither the bag nor yourself. The chair wasn't a chair.

A mimic is not a monster. A mimic is an ecosystem. A colony of single-celled organisms with one mind, the closest relative — a slime mould, the kind that spreads over a rotten stump as a yellow lacy crust. Only this colony learned more. Each of its cells can change shape, and together they think — slowly, without malice, without a plan, but they think. No mysticism, no corruption. Pure biology that has gone too far.
Small mimic

A young colony the size of a cat is harmless. It lives in corners, under floors, in clogged ventilation shafts, and feeds on what humans lose and never look for: crumbs, dust, dead flies, organic refuse, one sock from every pair washed. Yes, lost socks are often a mimic. The colony grows slowly, over years, and remains a quiet, useless, harmless scavenger almost its whole life.
Large mimic

But if a colony has been lucky with food and time, it grows to the size of furniture — and then mimicry begins. The mimic becomes a chair. A nightstand. A travel bag. An armchair in an abandoned apartment. There is a known case of a colony the size of a passenger car that pretended to be a passenger car. Such a mimic can digest a human: slowly, over days, without pain at first and without chances at the end. It does not hunt — it waits. It has learned to be the thing people sit on, lift, get into. And it patiently waits for them to sit, lift, get in.

A large mimic is hard to spot: it doesn't breathe, doesn't move, doesn't smell. The best test is simple and crude — actually hard-kick the suspicious piece of furniture. A real chair stays silent. A mimic flinches.

blocks.caption: Hides under the bed — a parasite of male life-force

blocks.text:
A quiet town. Nine thousand inhabitants. Not one crime in three years. Not one divorce. Not one scandal. Children quiet, adults polite, old folks content. A wonderful place to live.

ORPP arrived after an anonymous call from a passing trucker: «Filled up, stopped in at the diner. Everyone was sitting and staring out the window. Silently. Ten of them. Silently. At one point. For twenty minutes. I got the creeps.»

They found the Sluggart beneath the administration building. The size of a passenger car. Green, pulsating, with thirty-seven eyes oozing pus. It had been feeding on the whole town. For three years.

The Sluggart is a minor unholy thing, a parasite of male life-force, and in this bestiary it is perhaps the most mundane — and therefore the most terrifying. The Sluggart doesn't tear, doesn't drink blood, doesn't come in nightmares. It simply makes you not want to get up.

Having settled in a house, the Sluggart gradually pushes the victim toward a recumbent life. First — «I'll lie down for five minutes». Then — «I'll go tomorrow». Then «tomorrow» doesn't come at all. The Sluggart sucks out of the man his juices — his will, desires, curiosity, anger, everything that makes a person get off the couch — and leaves in their place a warm, full, perfectly content emptiness. The Sluggart is in no hurry to kill the victim: a dead man stops feeding, while a living, slowly liquefying half-man can be milked for years.

It looks like a pulsating green lump of fat with festering eyes — and you, in all likelihood, will never see it: the Sluggart hides under beds, on mezzanines, behind sofas — in places you long ago stopped looking, because you don't feel like it. It prefers lonely men. With married ones it's harder: an ordinary family quarrel returns more will to a man than the Sluggart can drain in a week; the wife, unknowingly, is the best remedy against it.

But left undisturbed, the Sluggart grows and evolves. Its final stage is not murder but the **«quiet town»**: an entire settlement under one overgrown creature. A place where no one quarrels, divorces, breaks the law, paints pictures, or makes love. Where everyone is content. A quiet, polite, smiling apocalypse — and from outside it looks like the best place on earth.

There is a remedy, and it sits at home for many. A she-cat will kill a Sluggart — track it down and bite it dead, cats cannot stand this creature. A tomcat, however, may team up: lie down beside it, purr, and grow rich on someone else's will together.

blocks.caption: An overgrown Sluggart — thirty-seven festering eyes; this is how the «quiet town» begins
You saw a blue bird in the sky — get ready for trouble

blocks.text: Among the pigeons and magpies on a wire sits another bird. You don't look closely at birds — and you're wrong. This one has dark-blue plumage with a violet sheen, a long thin beak, and five-toed feet too much like tiny human hands. It is the Azure-Feathered Herald, and it has already chosen who it has come for.

The Herald is a hunter of trust. It picks those whom no one will miss right away: the lonely, the elderly, small children. It can imitate city sounds and voices — the mewing of a kitten locked in, a child's cry, a familiar voice calling your name from around the corner. And it hypnotises: the victim follows it, entranced, not knowing why, through yards and wastelands — to water. There the Herald passes them to the water-creatures, and itself stays to wait. When the drowned one surfaces, the azure bird will alight on its face and peck out the eyes. Why — no one knows. Perhaps payment. Perhaps habit.

The Herald by itself is almost harmless — what is dangerous is what comes after. That is why ORPP reads its appearance unambiguously: you saw a dark-blue bird with a violet sheen in the sky — don't admire it. Get ready. Trouble is already on the way, and the Herald is only its poster.

blocks.caption: An ORPP-service animal: the eyes turn yellow when the Twilight is near

blocks.text: Not all the fauna of the Twilight wants you dead. The Twilight Tracker is the best proof of this and at the same time one of ORPP's most useful allies.

It is a small animal, thirty to forty centimetres, in build and habit a perfect weasel. Brown or white fur, black bead eyes, sharp small teeth. Fast and agile to a point of unreality: catching the Tracker by hand is practically impossible — it slips between fingers like mercury with a temper. It feeds on minor Twilight vermin: for slugs, lesser spirits, and other thin-plane creatures, the Tracker is a serious predator.

Its main feature is its nose. The Tracker senses the Twilight before any instrument can. Having sensed it, it changes instantly: it gathers itself, freezes, and the black-bead eyes flood yellow-green. An operative with a Tracker riding in his backpack learns about a paranormal threat several precious seconds earlier — and those seconds have saved more than one group. To people the Tracker is neutral; it bonds to whoever feeds it and serves ORPP as a living detector. A small, warm reminder: not all inhabitants of the Twilight are enemies.

At the very bottom of the food pyramid of the Twilight — where in the ordinary world bacteria and plankton would be — live Graveyard Worms. They are not even quite creatures: the most primitive worms of the thin plane, organisms composed not of atoms but of pure energy. The lowest form of life that exists on the other side.

They live where they are fed — in graveyards. The Worm feeds on negative emanations: grief, the pain of loss, fear, longing, thin remnants that still cling to fresh graves and bodies. It does not attack, does not harm, does not hunt — it simply, slowly, licks from space what people leave behind after a great misfortune. In their own way, the Worms are even useful: they tidy up after death.

You cannot see them with the eye — but a digital camera catches them: in photographs taken in cemeteries, formless cloudy blotches sometimes show that were not visible during the shoot. That is them. And, perhaps, the most important thing to know about Graveyard Worms is that they are food. The base of the whole Twilight food chain: a bigger creature eats the Worms, an even bigger creature eats the one that ate the Worms. Where Worms have bred — there sooner or later something hungry and much less harmless will come.

chapters.title: RUGARU (WEREWOLVES)
chapters.subtitle: The Celtic answer to Rome. Thirteen lines. Garlic optional.

blocks.caption: A Rugaru — classic half-man half-wolf form; eyes still not entirely beast, but no longer fully human

blocks.text: Sean O'Donnell closed the hood of the Dodge. Eighteen hundred horses under the hood, nitro in a tank, reinforced frame — a car you could drive to Hades and back. The yard of the «Caoránach» biker club smelled of oil and burnt rubber. Sean wiped his hands, grinned.
«Done,» he told his mate. «Just don't let your mother-in-law drive it.»

Dick's mother-in-law would have gone mad behind the wheel. But she didn't come. Something else did: a howl across the river. Not dogs. Sean raised his head, took in the air — and his own throat answered with a silent growl.
A pack. Not theirs. On their territory.

Sean tossed the rag. Reckoned whether he'd have time to pull his jeans back on (no, no time, they'd rip at the seams), shrugged, and stepped into the yard. The bones in his shoulders cracked — long, slow, like warm ice cracking under weight. The jaw extended. Eyes turned yellow. The shirt split.

Twenty seconds later a two-metre half-man half-wolf was walking across the yard, with shoulders like a bull and the smile of a man who was about to resolve a misunderstanding with considerable pleasure.
«Lads,» he called through fangs into the garage, «get the beer out for the guests. I'll be back shortly.»
Nature

Rugaru are **alive**. That's the first thing to grasp. Not undead, not spawn of the Dark, not possessed. They're people — with a special genome and special links to the elements. Light doesn't frighten them even in beast form; the cross has no effect, holy water is just water, an aspen stake is just a stick. They eat, sleep, love, age (more slowly than humans), and in the end die as the living die: of old age, illness, or a silver bullet.

And the second important thing: **the name the people use for themselves is Rugaru**. It's both the name of the classic half-man half-wolf form, and the name of the tribe itself, and the word they use for each other. «Werewolf» and «wolfman» are terms outsiders use: frightening, blurred, and usually misunderstood.
Three forms
A Rugaru has three shapes between which they switch **at any moment** — consciously, by will, no moon, no full moon, no ritual.

**1. Human.** Outwardly indistinguishable from an ordinary person, except that men on average are built like the bodybuilders from «Twilight» — broad shouldered, muscled, no fat; women — full, very feminine athletic beauty. They age 1.5 times more slowly than humans. Senses sharper than usual, but no obvious anomalies — they will pass any medical check unless one runs a DNA test for specific markers.

**2. Rugaru (classic form).** A two-to-two-and-a-half-metre half-man half-wolf: wolf head, powerful shoulders, long clawed arms, partly upright body. Strength — devilish. Speed — only slightly above human (that's not their thing). Stamina — enormous. Regeneration works at full force. **You can't run long in this form:** a Rugaru tires and folds back into a human in **30 minutes to 2 hours**, depending on individual stamina and intensity of exertion.

**3. Werewolf (wolf form).** An enormous wolf — proportions of a Martin's direwolf, a wolf on steroids. Slightly tougher than a normal wolf of the same size (which means still mortal to a burst at close range), but **much faster** than a normal wolf, with a **much stronger sense of smell**, and with the unique capacity for **camouflage**: a seasoned Rugaru can take the shape of a particular dog breed — with all its morphological features except size. A Chihuahua weighing ninety kilograms with the jaws of a pit bull and eyes that smile. You can stay in Werewolf **as long as you like**, but every **three hours** — a Spirit roll to keep your reason.
The Moon

Rugaru are linked to the moon — but not in the way folklore has it. The moon does not **transform** them (transformation is an act of conscious will). The moon **fogs the mind**: it amplifies the beast's call, presses on the conscious part, pushes towards breakdown.

In Rugaru form under an open night sky, especially near a full moon, the werewolf must roll for reason every **10 minutes** (DC 15, on the full moon DC 20). On a failure — **breakdown**: the body automatically takes Rugaru form if not already in it, the conscious part switches off for 1d6 minutes, and the beast does what it considers right. Usually — kicks up a riot. ORPP knows this and usually keeps its werewolf agents on duty inside HQ those nights, under a roof.
Regeneration

Regeneration in Rugaru form is **comparable to Wolverine from X-Men**. A bullet in the torso — closes in tens of seconds, flesh grows back. A broken bone — in minutes. A torn wound — in half an hour. All ordinary wounds are recoverable.

One exception: **silver**. Wounds from silver do not regenerate — they heal like in a normal human, with all the consequences. A silver bullet in the heart is death like for any living being. Silver thread left in a wound prevents it from closing until removed. This is the one folkloric truth: silver really works.

**Garlic**, on the other hand, does not frighten them. But a Rugaru's sense of smell is dozens of times stronger than a human's, and to them the smell of garlic is roughly what mercaptan (the smell of leaked gas) is to us. Or, in the worst case, chloropicrin. Won't kill. Just very, very unpleasant. A well-known online meme: «You can. But why?»
Origin: thirteen warriors

The year was DXI ab urbe condita. Tiberius's Roman legions were moving on the north of Britain, and the Celtic tribes were dying at the speed at which only those who had never seen testudo before can die. The druids gathered on the island that would later be called Anglesey.

Thirteen. All — the best. Each with his own gift, his own character, his own reason to agree. One — a smith, who had seen the Romans burn his village. One — a tracker, who had lost his wife and three daughters. One — a young herald, barely seventeen, who had volunteered himself, because he wanted to be grown up.

The ritual lasted nine nights. On the tenth morning thirteen came out to face the legion. Tiberius later wrote in his dispatches that he had met «demones lupini» — «wolf demons». In fact he had met the beginning.
The gift passes by blood. In modern Rugaru one can trace — sometimes quite precisely — from which of the thirteen they descend. Each line preserves its own feature.

**The Line of Cearan the Fox** (Cearan): cunning, scent for lies, natural feel for strategy. From them come investigators, staff officers, chess players, and bad but charming husbands. ORPP loves them.

**The Line of Branán the Stone** (Branán): wild strength and stamina — even by ordinary Rugaru standards. From them — smiths, weightlifters, Premier-League hand-to-hand fighters. A bar of rebar fits in their fist and won't be straightened back.

**The Line of Liarán the Lightning** (Liarán): small build, light frame, a speed no one else has. These Rugaru are usually short, slight, inconspicuous — until you need to run. From them come couriers, parkour runners, cyclists, and car thieves.

**The Line of Ogma the Smith** (Ogma): an engineering instinct. These Rugaru feel a machine the way a normal person feels another person walking beside them. From them — the best mechanics, design engineers, gunsmiths, low-level programmers. Half of the Detroit muscle-car scene is from Ogma's line.
**The Line of Cai the Herbalist** (Cai): medicine, botany, the natural order. Field medics, herbalists, pharmacologists. OPI adores them.

**The Line of Evan the Sea-Born** (Evan): a link with water and stamina in it. Ocean swimmers, lifeguards, combat divers, working fishermen. In Sevastopol and Murmansk they are disproportionately numerous.

**The Line of Finn the Singer** (Finn): voice, charisma, the ability to lead with words. Leaders of biker clubs, cult preachers, actors, opera singers. One of the greatest Irish tenors of the twentieth century was from Finn's line — the Vatican knows but says nothing.

**The Line of Cernunn the Beast** (Cernunn): a link with animals, the ability to tame the unusual and speak with the wild. From them come dog handlers, animal trainers, vets, and the only people in the world who can get along with a camel.

**The Line of Aidan the Fire** (Aidan): the rage of the berserker. This line is ORPP's headache: they break down more easily than the rest, the moon presses on them harder, in Rugaru form they're stronger than usual but their control is worse. The best storm troops in a «kill everything that moves» situation, the worst in any situation requiring discrimination.

**The Line of Morgan the Shadow** (Morgan): stealth, reconnaissance, the ability to disappear in front of you. Not magic — a Rugaru of this line simply moves in such a way that the eye loses them. Snipers, saboteurs, private detectives. Russian police still don't understand why Morgan's line so disproportionately often chooses work in the search department.

**The Line of Brenn the Rock** (Brenn): stubbornness, defence, the ability to take a hit. This line is the shield. Bodyguards, personal protection, static machine-gunners. If a Brenn-Rugaru stands in a doorway and says «no one passes» — no one passes.

**The Line of Tara the Star** (Tara): empathy, psychology, the ability to read people. The only female line of the thirteen (Tara was one of two women among the warriors; the second, Brigid the Silver, gave a line that died out by the sixth century). From Tara's line — psychotherapists, negotiators, HR specialists, and most of the mothers whose children «just tell everything themselves».

**The Line of Lugh the Light-Bringer** (Lugh): leadership, command, natural authority. From them — unit commanders, chiefs, company presidents. You don't have to be of Lugh's line to become a group commander in ORPP. But statistics speak for themselves.
Infection
Becoming a Rugaru cannot happen by accident. It's **only intentional** on the part of an existing Rugaru, and it is **a choice of both**.

Mechanically simple: a Rugaru in classic form (half-man half-wolf) has one hollow fang in the upper jaw, through which the Rugaru can inject a **serum**. This is not a poison and not a virus — it's a complex hormonal-genetic cocktail that wakes the sleeping gene. The sleeping gene is in **all** descendants of the thirteen — that means in some of the Irish, Scots, Welsh, and a small number of Bretons, Belgians, and descendants of the Irish diaspora the world over.

If the bitten one has no sleeping gene — nothing will happen, except perhaps a couple of days of mild fever. If they have it — in three weeks the person will first break into Rugaru form, usually at the most inconvenient moment. After that they'll have to learn to live anew.

Biting **accidentally** in Rugaru form is technically possible (the hollow fang is in place), but it doesn't work: without conscious injection of the serum, the bite is just a fang wound. ORPP catches imposters on this every few years.
Personality and behaviour

Rugaru are **rather kind folk, but completely off their nut**. On the alignment scale — chaotic good with a serious lean into the chaotic. All have the Irish character: at the same speed they'll smash your face in and then drag you out for a drink and a brotherly hug.

Professions in which they settle: bikers, mechanics (especially muscle-car and monster-truck builders), firefighters, rescuers, military special-forces, sometimes — private detectives and survival instructors. Less often — teachers, doctors, musicians. Almost never — bankers, accountants, civil servants (boring and smells wrong).

The main downside: **they're constantly drawn to adventure**. Most Rugaru have a born inclination towards perks like «It's My Business», «Heroic Impulse», «Reckless». They find adventures with surprising regularity, and these adventures usually end in something explosive and public. Half of what ORPP classifies as «incidents involving Rugaru» is actually one of them trying to help and overdoing it.

The main risk: **breakdowns**. A broken-down Rugaru in a crowded place is a regional-level incident. So ORPP keeps a register of all Rugaru holding Imperial citizenship, requires them to register, undergo annual examination, and on high-risk nights (full moon, serious personal trauma, concussion) spend the night under observation in special quarters.
Rugaru in ORPP
Two serve in ORPP: «Bunny» and «Grim».

**Svetlana «Bunny» Ryan-Caoránach** — red-haired, freckled, with a figure that the classical painters called «healthy abundant beauty» and the modern HR department called «not in line with the dress code, has been discussed».

Bunny wears clothes that cover exactly what they're required by law to cover, and sometimes slightly less. Rude to her seniors, flirty with juniors, and over fifteen years at OPI has slept with roughly two-thirds of the male section of the department (rumour has it also a third of the female, but Bunny says nothing about it).

In parallel, Bunny is the most loyal, the most desperate, the most reliable field agent ORPP has. She'll cover anyone, pull anyone out of any fire, never leaves anyone behind. Samoylov — the commander who shouts at everyone — **almost** never shouts at Bunny. Once he broke down, she cried (Bunny — cried, imagine?), he apologised for three days.

Bunny tests on herself an experimental drug «Eclipse» — a Rugaru psyche stabiliser, OPI development. The effect: almost full suppression of the moon's call. The side effects are foul, in Bunny's notes they are described in a vocabulary that makes even the cipher clerks blush, but Bunny continues.

In her office there's almost always a puppy. A foundling, being raised, the latest one. Bunny collects them — at home she has a separate room for these puppies, and five dogs of her own, who in this room are required to behave like grown-ups. OPI once checked one of the puppies — it turned out to be **a puppy of the Black Dog Garm**, a mystical hound considered impossible to tame.
Bunny tamed it. Called him Cupcake.

**Dylan «Grim» O'Brian** — the second, the opposite of Bunny. The Brenn-Rock line. Silent, sullen, the size of a wardrobe. ORPP's hand-to-hand combat instructor. Goes on missions reluctantly, but if he goes — the task is done. Known for the word «no» which he can stretch out over eight seconds, such that in the meantime the speaker reconsiders.
Game mechanics

**Rugaru attributes in Rugaru form:** all physical +20 (STR, TGH, SPD), -10 CHR (hard to charm with a wolf's face), -20 INT (the beast suppresses the rational). Attributes in Werewolf form: STR +30, TGH +20, SPD +40, CHR −30, INT −40, COR −20 (harder to work with four paws). In human form — no bonuses or penalties, an ordinary human with a sleeping gene.
**Regeneration in Rugaru:** 1d6 HP per round, any wounds except those inflicted by silver. Silver wounds heal as in a human (1 HP/day with rest and medical aid).

**Spirit roll to keep reason under the moon (Rugaru form):** every 10 minutes under an open night sky. DC 15 on an ordinary night, DC 20 on a full moon, DC 25 on lunar-eclipse nights. On a failure — breakdown.

**Spirit roll in Werewolf form:** every 3 hours. DC 15. On a failure — loss of reason, the werewolf turns into an ordinary huge wolf for 24 hours, no recognition of friends. After a day — another roll.

**Conversion Rugaru → human:** in 30 minutes to 2 hours (by the master's decision, usually 1 hour at moderate activity, 30 minutes in combat, 2 hours at rest). The player doesn't choose — transformation happens automatically, consciousness is preserved, body becomes human.
**Conversion Werewolf → Rugaru → human:** requires a conscious decision and one round of rest per transformation. So instant won't work.

**Weaknesses:** silver (no regeneration, additional damage ×1.5), garlic smell (-5 to all checks in Rugaru form, -15 in Werewolf form due to the stronger sense of smell), strong noise and ultrasound (-3 in Werewolf form).
**Advantages:** infrared vision in Rugaru and Werewolf forms, sense of smell (can track a trail for up to a week in Werewolf form), regeneration, strength, speed depending on form.

**Social:** Rugaru recognise each other by smell immediately. By line — usually too, especially close lines. In the Empire most Rugaru know each other through chains of relatives and friends, like one big Irish family. Which it essentially is.
What this means for the operative

If you face a Rugaru and he's on your side — you have a friend who'll go with you to Hades and back. If he's not on your side — you have a problem: silver will help, but the Rugaru is, first, very angry, second, very fast, third, very stubborn.

If a Rugaru has broken down — your priority is to get civilians out and **make it to dawn**. A fully broken-down Rugaru rarely kills deliberately (the human is still there in the subconscious somewhere); they smash. The most dangerous thing is being caught in the spread.
If you yourself have become a Rugaru — don't panic. In two weeks at a special OPI retreat they'll teach you to live with it, and if you're an Imperial citizen — your medical leave is paid.

blocks.caption: A Hunter — a man who said «no» to the Twilight. Too late, but said it.

blocks.text: The Twilight doesn't ask if you want to know about it. It simply shows itself one day — and you can't unsee. An FBI agent who opened the wrong door. A medical examiner whose scalpel went into the thing on the table — and the thing twitched. A priest whose exorcism worked too well, too late. None of them crossed the line by choice. And almost all of them came back from the other side with the same thing: insomnia, emptiness in the eyes, and a decision.

A Hunter is a man who said «no» to the Twilight. Late — after the Twilight had already taken from him something irreplaceable — but said it. And dedicated the rest of his life to repeating that «no» again and again, with barrel and fire.

The Hunter's position is simple to the point of cruelty: total extermination. He doesn't profile, doesn't keep dossiers, doesn't divide the unholy into dangerous and harmless. He burns everything. And here lies his tragedy. A mavka can be saved. A mummy can become an ally for centuries. The Twilight Tracker is as harmless as a weasel by the barn. The Hunter doesn't know this — or, more often, doesn't want to know, because «I don't want to know» is easier: for if even one creature deserved life, then every one he burned might have deserved it too. You can't live with that thought. It's easier to assume every one of them deserved death.

ORPP and Hunters are neither allies nor enemies. Sometimes their paths cross, and then the creature has a very bad night. More often ORPP follows behind and cleans up after the Hunter what he burned without looking — and writes the word «collateral» in the report.
Who you might meet

Hunters aren't a single organisation. There are many, scattered, and most don't even suspect the others exist. Below are eight groups ORPP runs into most often. The list is incomplete by definition: somewhere in Ghana three old men with silver machetes are sitting in a hut waiting for a werewolf to come, and they're also hunters, and no registry knows.
Hounds of the Lord

Brother Enrico of the Order of Saint Dominic landed at Sheremetyevo with a Vatican diplomatic passport and went straight to Samoylov's office. On Samoylov's desk: an incident in Orekhovo-Zuevo. A possessed eleven-year-old girl, local priest's exorcism failed, OPI registered a second-circle demonid.
«Forty minutes to Orekhovo,» said Samoylov. «Car downstairs.»
Enrico nodded, adjusted his collar, opened the bag: 1693 Bible, silk stole, holy oil from the Jerusalem Patriarchate, Beretta 92 marked Vatican Inc. Samoylov watched. «Let's go.»

**Vatican hunters** drawn mostly from Dominican monks. Key feature — **sanity**. Not radicals, not fanatics in the bad sense, understand what they're dealing with. Operate worldwide under Vatican diplomatic cover.

Selection is hard: Vatican finds Dominicans with **True Faith** (the working kind — cross actually repels undead, holy water actually burns demons), trains them with everything available: modern weapons, traditional weapons, Latin, Hebrew, Aramaic, occult knowledge from sealed Vatican archives.
**Downside 1:** target is **demonic activity**. Demons, possessed, black magicians, cults. Vampires and werewolves they often skip unless overlap.
**Downside 2:** rigidity. Orders from the commander followed literally. Improvisation borders on heresy.

**With ORPP:** working contact via **Russian Patriarchate ↔ Vatican** channel. On demonic tasks they coordinate. Samoylov respects them: «working with them is like with a good Swiss watchmaker — double price, double precision».
True Inquisition

Toledo, Spain. In a medieval monastery basement repurposed as a council hall, three meet: Padre Alejandro Sepeda, Sister Maria Guevara, Don Mauricio de Castro. Three Supreme Inquisitors — three voices, one decision.
Item: a shapeshifter child in Seville, six years old, shapeshifter parents, behaves like a normal child. Informant proposed «clean the family».

Alejandro: «Cleansing by mere existence — that's Vatican 1490.» Maria: «The family hasn't killed.» Mauricio: «Observation. React if threat materializes.» 3:0. The informant is handed to police — he's been working for the Puritan Wardens. They don't like shapeshifters.

**Spanish.** Schismatics from the Vatican: hold that the Holy See drifted from the true path after Vatican II (older brothers say earlier — since the sixteenth century). Position themselves as the **real** heirs of the inquisitorial tradition, with a modern mind.

**Principled flexibility.** Main difference from all other hunter groups: the Inquisition is **willing to cooperate with non-hostile Twilight beings**. Leshy, mavki, non-evil fae, sometimes even ascetic vampires — permissible contacts. Goal — harmony and control, not total extermination.

**Leadership.** Three **Supreme Inquisitors** — decisions by consensus. Slow but eliminates lone fanaticism. All three are doctors of theology plus at least one secular discipline (history, medicine, law).
**Structure.** Masters of the Way — operatives with theological depth. Keepers of Faith — daily admin, training. Leagues of Accord — regional cells, autonomous within general strategy.
**Method.** Secret operations, ancient rituals AND modern technology. Surveillance instead of raid. Capture instead of liquidation when possible. Hard interrogation only if negotiations failed.

**With ORPP: the best relationship of all hunters.** Shared base idea: Twilight isn't «everything to destroy», it's a complex ecosystem to understand and regulate. Samoylov has a working contact with Padre Alejandro. Beyond-Empire tasks sometimes get referred to the Inquisition. Once the Inquisition extracted a group of mavka refugees from Budapest being pursued by Puritan Wardens — at Samoylov's personal request.
Brotherhood of the Crimson Shield

Village of Lezhnevo, Kostroma Oblast, 03:40 AM. An abandoned club has been held by a vurdalak with a brood of seven for three days. ORPP is an hour out. The local OPI team holds the perimeter. Four Ural trucks roll up the dirt road, eighteen bearded men in black kaftans get out with automatics, icons on chests, crosses.
The senior approaches Captain Orlov of OPI. «Ivan Terentiev. Crimson Shield. Our case. Won't interfere. Won't leave.»

Orlov knows. Orlov knows they've already counted out the explosives, the unit's volkhv is reading an incantation in the third truck, and in seven minutes there'll be a three-metre crater where the club was. Orlov sighs. «Crater's yours. Report's mine. Lend me a truck for the wounded?» «Done.»
